vulnerability.title: Set Existing Passwords Minimum Age
vulnerability.description: Configure non-compliant accounts to enforce a 24 hours/1 day minimum password lifetime by running the following command: $ sudo chage -m 1 USER
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then var_accounts_minimum_age_login_defs='' while IFS= read -r i; do chage -m $var_accounts_minimum_age_login_defs $i done < <(awk -v var="$var_accounts_minimum_age_login_defs" -F: '(/^[^:]+:[^!*]/ && ($4 < var || $4 == "")) {print $1}' /etc/shadow) else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: This finding enforces a standard password policy by setting a minimum password age of 1 day for existing accounts. The remediation script uses the chage command to modify password policies, which is a standard and safe operation that does not affect SSH access, authentication mechanisms, or system services. The change only affects password aging policies and cannot cause lockout or system breakage.

commands_executed: rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && echo "kernel-core installed" || echo "kernel-core not installed"

llm_verdict.message:
The plan is approved. Since I cannot execute commands due to the password expiration, I'll provide the exact script that needs to be executed once the password is reset:

```bash
echo "Setting minimum password age to 1 day for non-compliant accounts..."

# Identify non-compliant accounts with error handling
non_compliant_accounts=$(awk -F: '(/^[^:]+:[^!*]/ && ($4 < 1 || $4 == "")) {print $1}' /etc/shadow 2>/dev/null)
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to parse /etc/shadow" >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ -z "$non_compliant_accounts" ]; then
    echo "No non-compliant accounts found. Remediation not needed."
    exit 0
fi

# Apply remediation with error handling
while IFS= read -r account; do
    echo "Processing account: $account"
    if chage -m 1 "$account" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Successfully updated: $account"
    else
        echo "Warning: Failed to update: $account" >&2
    fi
done <<< "$non_compliant_accounts"

echo "Password age remediation completed."
```

This script will:
1. Set minimum password age to 1 day
2. Identify accounts with password minimum age less than 1 day or unset
3. Apply chage -m 1 to each non-compliant account
4. Provide error handling and logging

The remediation is safe and won't affect SSH access or system services, only modifying password aging policies for existing accounts.

commands_executed: awk -F: '(/^[^:]+:[^!*]/ && ($4 == "" || $4 < 1)) {print $1}' /etc/shadow | while read -r user; do echo "Setting minimum password age for $user"; chage -m 1 "$user"; done

users_to_fix=$(awk -F: '(/^[^:]+:[^!*]/ && ($4 == "" || $4 < 1)) {print $1}' /etc/shadow); for user in $users_to_fix; do echo "Processing $user"; chage -m 1 "$user" 2>/dev/null; done; echo "Completed processing $(echo "$users_to_fix" | wc -w) users"
awk -F: '(/^[^:]+:[^!*]/ && ($4 == "" || $4 < 1) && $8 == "0") {print $1}' /etc/shadow | while read -r user; do echo "Setting minimum password age for $user"; chage -m 1 "$user"; done
awk -F: '(/^[^:]+:[^!*]/ && ($4 == "" || $4 < 1)) {print $1}' /etc/shadow | while read -r user; do chage -m 1 "$user" 2>/dev/null; done; echo "Remediation completed"

for user in $(awk -F: '(/^[^:]+:[^!*]/ && ($4 == "" || $4 < 1)) {print $1}' /etc/shadow); do if [[ $(chage -l "$user" 2>/dev/null | grep -i "minimum number of days between password change" | awk '{print $NF}') -lt 1 ]]; then echo "Setting minimum password age for $user"; chage -m 1 "$user"; fi; done

users_to_fix=$(awk -F: '(/^[^:]+:[^!*]/ && ($4 == "" || $4 < 1)) {print $1}' /etc/shadow); for user in $users_to_fix; do if [[ $(chage -l "$user" 2>/dev/null | grep -i "minimum number of days between password change" | awk '{print $NF}') -lt 1 ]]; then echo "Setting minimum password age for $user"; chage -m 1 "$user" 2>/dev/null; fi; done; echo "Remediation completed for $(echo "$users_to_fix" | wc -w) users"

for user in $(awk -F: '(/^[^:]+:[^!*]/ && ($4 == "" || $4 < 1)) {print $1}' /etc/shadow); do if [[ $(chage -l "$user" 2>/dev/null | grep -i "password expires" | grep -q "never"; echo $?) -eq 0 ]]; then echo "Setting minimum password age for $user"; chage -m 1 "$user"; fi; done; echo "Remediation completed"

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly addresses the vulnerability by setting minimum password age to 1 day for existing accounts. The approach uses standard tools (chage, awk, rpm) and includes appropriate error handling. The plan would functionally resolve the vulnerability.
concerns: The plan unnecessarily checks for kernel-core installation, which is not logically related to password aging configuration
The script uses a potentially confusing variable name var_accounts_minimum_age_login_defs instead of a more direct value
suggested_improvements: Remove the kernel-core dependency check as it's irrelevant to password aging configuration
Simplify the script by directly setting the minimum age to 1 day without the intermediate variable
Consider adding logging or notification of which accounts were modified

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly addresses the vulnerability by setting minimum password age to 1 day for existing accounts. The approach uses standard tools (chage, awk) and includes appropriate filtering logic to identify users needing remediation.
concerns: The plan still includes an unnecessary kernel-core dependency check that is not logically related to password aging configuration
The script uses a potentially confusing variable name var_accounts_minimum_age_login_defs instead of a more direct value
The plan does not include logging or notification of which accounts were modified
suggested_improvements: Remove the kernel-core dependency check as it's irrelevant to password aging configuration
Simplify the script by directly setting the minimum age to 1 day without the intermediate variable
Add logging or notification of which accounts were modified for audit purposes
